title: Mission run-record
description: Curated, schema-validated record of one mission, WRITTEN by the orchestrator (never synthesized by a second model — constitution §7). Lands in claude-fieldnotes/mission_records/<run-id>.json. The evolution loop reads these; a record is the only valid evidence for an amendment proposal. v0.2: defines nodes_executed (v0.1 required it but never defined it — unsatisfiable under additionalProperties:false), adopts the v0.3-constitution telemetry blocks (compute_tiers §3.6, r_tier_escape_outcomes §7, escalation_precision §3.3, budget_planned_vs_actual §6, audit), and adds token_budget/agent_budget to the cap_hits enum. Validate at report time with scripts/validate_record.py — deterministic, no model call (§1.3).

schema_version.description: New records MUST write "0.2". "0.1" is accepted only so legacy records stay parseable; the validator treats 0.1 as warn-only legacy.

constitution_version.description: Governing version. REQUIRED — the evolution loop must not compare records across regimes (§7).

mission_class.description: Orchestration depth the mission ran at (§2.4). Recorded for classification calibration.

nodes_executed.description: One entry per node the executor closed (or failed to). The per-node ground truth the calibration corpus keys on.

closed_by.description: What closed the node: check = V0/V1 closure record, critic = V2 verdict, human = V3, harness = deterministic machinery.

model_tier.description: Compute tier the actor ran at (§3.6). Optional until all executors emit it.

classification_calibration.description: The classification-observer corpus (§7 classification calibration) — the record-and-match signal for the V-class and M-class dials. TRUTH-SOURCE ASYMMETRY (§2.2/§2.3) is enforced by the `may_lower` flags: a critic/auditor opinion may only license RAISING a class or flagging for the human; only a human_diff or machine_check may license LOWERING one. The matcher that consumes this corpus is deferred until same-version N is large (evolution.md).

mission_class_assigned_note.description: Classifier-floor vs final class rationale (e.g. judgment raises per §2.4).

mission_class_verdict.description: Hindsight on whether the mission was over- or under-provisioned.

may_lower.description: MUST be false unless evidence_source is human_diff or machine_check. A critic_opinion can never set this true (§2.2 asymmetry) — it may only flag for the human or argue UP.

node_class_verdicts.description: Per-node V-class hindsight. A node escalated to a critic that found nothing, or a V2 node the human-diff left untouched, is an over-classification candidate — but only human/machine evidence may license down-classifying that node-shape next time.

features.description: The shape signal a future matcher keys on — recorded NOW so the corpus has signal (§7, record-now-match-later). Globs + applicable-verifier are the strong signals; instruction_gist is weak/secondary.

path_globs.description: Globs/paths the node touched — the primary shape signal.

applicable_verifier.description: Repo-contract verifier-registry entry that applied; null = no machine check exists (a strong V2 signal).

planner_first_class.description: Class the planner first reached for BEFORE §2.2 round-up — captures the rounding decision the matcher can learn to keep or override.

instruction_gist.description: Short normalized task description; weak signal, secondary to globs + verifier.

plan_vs_executed.description: How reality diverged from the frozen plan — the raw material for cap/rule calibration.

cap_hits.description: Every node that hit a cap — AND every mission-level budget-ceiling hit (§6 dual ceiling: use node "mission" with cap token_budget or agent_budget). A breach that only appears as report prose is invisible to calibration; it MUST land here. The audit's later judgment (would_have_converged) is the calibration signal.

node.description: Node id, or the sentinel "mission" for mission-level budget caps.

would_have_converged.description: Audit judgment: was the cap-hit effort-shaped (true → raise candidate) or structural (false → cap was right)?

fight.description: FIGHT-phase summary line (lenses, rounds, blockers/majors/minors, waivers).

compute_tiers.description: §3.6 model-tier telemetry: the FREEZE histogram, recorded as-executed. Validates each tier on evidence (§7) — a cheap tier that leaks defects past its gate tightens on the next calibration.

gates.description: Tier the gating critics / cold verifier ran at (constitution floor: strongest).

floored_up.description: Below-floor requests the executor floored up (§3.6) — each is a planner-calibration signal.

r_tier_escape_outcomes.description: §7 escape-rate telemetry per review tier: did a defect pass this node's review and get caught later (or never)? The evidence that validates/invalidates each R-tier assignment.

review_tier.description: R0-R3; combined ladders allowed (e.g. "R2/R3" when a panel followed a cold-eye).

escape.description: Free-form but prefer the vocabulary: none | yes-caught-downstream | yes-caught-at-audit | yes-escaped-to-delivery.

escalation_precision.description: §3.3 escalation-precision telemetry: of the blockers escalated to the human, how many were legit vs noise? legit/noise stay null until /mission-log-audit fills them from the human verdicts.

budget_planned_vs_actual.description: §6 mission budget (dual ceiling, frozen at PLAN) vs actual. exhausted=true means graceful convergence was triggered. Any overrun MUST also appear in plan_vs_executed.cap_hits (node "mission").

note.description: Planned-vs-actual commentary (e.g. FIGHT estimate accuracy) — a calibration signal for the feasibility lens.

audit.description: AUDIT-phase summary: what was re-run / sampled and the outcome. Lenient sub-shape — the audit narrative varies by mission class.

defect_ledger.description: Known shortfalls in the delivered artifact (§5).

human_review.description: Filled by /mission-log-audit when the Human reviews. THE GOLD SIGNAL (§7): the diff between delivered and accepted is ground truth for where the framework misjudged.

human_diff_stat.description: git diff --stat between delivered tree and accepted (post-edit) tree. Large diff on a node the framework self-closed = a verification gap.

human_diff_summary.description: Short human/orchestrator note on WHAT changed and why — the categorized lesson.

blocker_verdicts.description: Escalation-precision telemetry: was each human-escalated blocker legit or noise? A falling legit-rate is evidence for tightening critic prompts (§3.3).

accepted.description: Did the Human merge the agent branch (with or without edits)?
